Okay, what's up guys and girls? Being able to overcome obstacles and help people make a decision is one of the most valuable pieces of being in sales. It's it is persuasion, it's using influence, it's using humor, sarcasm, anything that you can to basically I mean to influence them to make a decision and really honestly you have to see this as help. Okay you have to see this as helping someone because most people are just afraid. They're afraid of making a mistake. They're afraid of trying something and feeling which will make them feel like their status. You see 30 of them will buy this because their buying threshold is above the actual price point. So they came in pretty much ready to buy and as long as the price is like reasonable to them which 500 bucks for a lot of people is for what they're getting from a value standpoint do is to sign up because this is like this is gonna be this is gonna be the vacation they've been wanting and we're gonna take them there. Alright so that's the reason for the obstacles and the three main categories of types of obstacles that my wife I need to talk to my spouse any of those related things are gonna be the second type like oh I'd love to do it but I have to talk to someone else right. The third one is resources. I don't have the money to do this program. I'd love to it sounds amazing but I don't have the money. Alright none of them are the kiss of death. You just got to know how to overcome them know how to frame it relative to other things and all of a sudden it becomes really clear that I need to take action today and sign up. Alright so without further ado the obstacle handling sequence.
